content.problemStatement:
There is an **empty bus** with MMM seats and a total of NNN people, numbered from 111 to NNN. Everyone is currently outside the bus. You are given a sequence of QQQ events of the following form.



+ i+\ i+ i :  It denotes that the person iii enters the bus.




− i-\ i− i : It denotes that the person iii leaves the bus.



It is guaranteed in the input that each person from 111 to NNN enters at most once as well as leaves the bus at most once.

Determine whether the sequence of events is consistent or not (i.e. no person leaves the bus before entering and the number of passengers in the bus does not exceed MMM at any point of time).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains Q+1Q + 1Q+1 lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N,M,QN, M, QN,M,Q.
QQQ lines follow. For each valid jjj, jthj^{th}jth of these lines contains a character chchch, followed by a space and an integer iii. Here chchch is either '+++' or '−-−' and 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.
It is guaranteed that ‘‘+``+‘‘+ i"i"i" and ‘‘−``-‘‘− i"i"i" appears at most once for every 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one string - "Consistent" (without quotes) if the sequence is consistent, "Inconsistent" (without quotes) otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: After Person 222 enters the bus, there are two people inside the bus while the capacity of the bus is 111.

Explanation:
Test case 111: Person 333 leaves the bus without entering and thus it is inconsistent.
